title: leonotis velutina var. rugosa
intro: Leonotis velutina var. rugosa, also known as 'Wild Dagga', is a perennial plant native to South Africa, belonging to the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its hairy, velvety leaves and small, tubular flowers. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of this plant have been used for their medicinal properties, exhibiting antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Leonotis velutina var. rugosa include its potential to reduce anxiety and stress, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used in traditional medicine and natural health practices.
